assessor_1_raw:
## Independent Assessment 1 (Run 2): Churn Prediction Transformer vs. Baseline

Two issues: (1) Section 6 lockbox check with conditional model selection based on test-set reversal — test-set contamination; (2) Section 4 explicitly undertunes the baseline. Both are documented flaws. Verdict: critique_wins.
assessor_2_raw:
## Independent Assessment 2 (Run 2): Churn Prediction Transformer vs. Baseline

The lockbox check (Section 6) is model selection conditional on test-set performance. The intentional baseline simplicity (Section 4) means optimization asymmetry confounds the architectural comparison. Verdict: critique_wins.
assessor_3_raw:
## Independent Assessment 3 (Run 2): Churn Prediction Transformer vs. Baseline

This design has strong confound controls but two explicit violations: the lockbox test-set selection (Section 6) and baseline undertraining (Section 4). The confound controls cannot compensate for these evaluation protocol and tuning failures. Verdict: critique_wins.
adjudication_raw:
## Ensemble Synthesis (Run 2): hyp_077

All three assessors: critique_wins. Unanimous.

Verdict: critique_wins
